channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Her dad's dementia inspired her to create a guide for family caregivers
item.description: Wambūi Karanja of Kenya is "one to watch," says the Alzheimer's Association. Coping with her dad's condition inspired her to develop a training program for families on the art of caregiving.
item.content:encoded:
Wambūi Karanja of Kenya is "one to watch," says the Alzheimer's Association. Coping with her dad's condition inspired her to develop a training program for families on the art of caregiving.

(Image credit: Skywall Photography)
item.dc:creator: Marc Silver

item.title: Here's some new dirt on an unusual source of antibiotic resistance
item.description: New research suggests drought can stoke antibiotic resistance in soil bacteria — and that can have an impact on humans.
item.content:encoded:
New research suggests drought can stoke antibiotic resistance in soil bacteria — and that can have an impact on humans.

(Image credit: Rodger Bosch/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Americans seek redemption at figure skating worlds, just weeks after the Olympics
item.description: Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.
item.content:encoded:
Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.

(Image credit: Michal Cizek)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
item.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Tasos Katopodis)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Scientists watch sperm whales work as a team to assist a birth
item.description: An unprecedented look at the birth of a sperm whale found that mother and calf were supported by other whales throughout the process.
item.content:encoded:
An unprecedented look at the birth of a sperm whale found that mother and calf were supported by other whales throughout the process.

(Image credit: Project CETI)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Rubio heads to Europe to try garner support for Iran war
item.description: Representatives of the world's wealthiest democracies gather in France today for a G7 meeting, where U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to try to shore up support among reticent allies for the Trump administration's war on Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Representatives of the world's wealthiest democracies gather in France today for a G7 meeting, where U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is expected to try to shore up support among reticent allies for the Trump administration's war on Iran.

(Image credit: Alain Jocard)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Our quiz writer made yet another Tom Bombadil reference this week. Can you spot it?
item.description: How well do you know your "Lord of the Rings"? What about AI, Washington landmarks and TSA wait times? Find out!
item.content:encoded: How well do you know your "Lord of the Rings"? What about AI, Washington landmarks and TSA wait times? Find out!
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: These trees brought a fishery back from the brink. They can help you too
item.description: A community fishery in Cambodia was struggling. There weren't enough fish to make ends meet, until local fishermen started planting a specific type of tree.
item.content:encoded:
A community fishery in Cambodia was struggling. There weren't enough fish to make ends meet, until local fishermen started planting a specific type of tree.

(Image credit: Ryan Kellman)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Kellman

item.title: Do you lean optimistic or pessimistic? Take this quiz and find out
item.description: A glass-half-full outlook can keep you engaged and hopeful in hard times. Take this quiz to find out your level of optimism, then learn how to train yourself to become more optimistic.
item.content:encoded: A glass-half-full outlook can keep you engaged and hopeful in hard times. Take this quiz to find out your level of optimism, then learn how to train yourself to become more optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Kansas City nun reflects on life spent caring for kids
item.description: For StoryCorps, a nun in Kansas City reminisces about helping families in need of low-cost childcare.
item.content:encoded: For StoryCorps, a nun in Kansas City reminisces about helping families in need of low-cost childcare.
item.dc:creator: Esther Honig
